Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

Name: Ivysaur
Flavor-Text: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

Name: Venusaur
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

Name: Venusaur ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

Name: Caterpie
Effects: Put 1 random [G] Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antenna on its head to drive away enemies.

Name: Metapod
Flavor-Text: It is waiting for the moment to evolve. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

Name: Butterfree
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

Name: Weedle
Flavor-Text: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

Name: Kakuna
Flavor-Text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

Name: Beedrill
Flavor-Text: It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.

Name: Oddish
Flavor-Text: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

Name: Gloom
Flavor-Text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

Name: Vileplume
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

Name: Paras
Flavor-Text: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

Name: Parasect
Flavor-Text: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

Name: Venonat
Flavor-Text: Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches small bug Pokémon at night that are attracted by light.

Name: Venomoth
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.

Name: Bellsprout
Flavor-Text: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

Name: Weepinbell
Flavor-Text: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

Name: Victreebel
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, once during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

Name: Exeggcute
Flavor-Text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

Name: Exeggutor ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Scyther
Flavor-Text: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

Name: Cottonee
Flavor-Text: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

Name: Whimsicott
Flavor-Text: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it’ll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

Name: Lilligant
Effects: Take a [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [G] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

Name: Skiddo
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the backs of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.

Name: Gogoat
Flavor-Text: It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

Name: Charmeleon
Flavor-Text: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

Name: Charizard
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

Name: Charizard ex
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.
Flavor-Text: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

Name: Arcanine ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

Name: Rapidash
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Discard 1 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Moltres
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It’s one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

Name: Moltres ex
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Take an amount of [R] Energy from your Energy Zone equal to the number of heads and attach it to your Benched [R] Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Heatmor
Flavor-Text: There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

Name: Salandit
Flavor-Text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

Name: Salazzle
Flavor-Text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

Name: Sizzlipede
Flavor-Text: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

Name: Squirtle
Flavor-Text: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

Name: Wartortle
Flavor-Text: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

Name: Blastoise
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

Name: Blastoise ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

Name: Golduck
Flavor-Text: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

Name: Poliwag
Flavor-Text: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.

Name: Poliwhirl
Flavor-Text: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

Name: Poliwrath
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.

Name: Tentacool
Flavor-Text: Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.

Name: Seel
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don’t bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

Name: Dewgong
Flavor-Text: It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.

Name: Shellder
Flavor-Text: It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

Name: Cloyster
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes -10 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

Name: Krabby
Flavor-Text: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

Name: Kingler
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

Name: Horsea
Flavor-Text: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

Name: Seadra
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

Name: Goldeen
Flavor-Text: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

Name: Seaking
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

Name: Staryu
Flavor-Text: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

Name: Starmie
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

Name: Magikarp
Flavor-Text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

Name: Lapras
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Omanyte
Flavor-Text: Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.

Name: Omastar
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: Weighed down by a large and heavy shell, Omastar couldn’t move very fast. Some say it went extinct because it was unable to catch food.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that this Pokémon’s beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

Name: Articuno ex
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Ducklett
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

Name: Swanna
Flavor-Text: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

Name: Froakie
Flavor-Text: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

Name: Frogadier
Flavor-Text: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise  control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

Name: Greninja
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may do 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water.  When it spins them and throws them at high speed,  these stars can split metal in two.

Name: Pyukumuku
Flavor-Text: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

Name: Bruxish
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

Name: Snom
Flavor-Text: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

Name: Frosmoth
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.

Name: Pikachu
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Pikachu ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Voltorb
Flavor-Text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

Name: Electrode
Flavor-Text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

Name: Zapdos ex
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

Name: Blitzle
Flavor-Text: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

Name: Zebstrika
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

Name: Tynamo
Flavor-Text: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

Name: Eelektrik
Flavor-Text: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

Name: Helioptile
Flavor-Text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

Name: Pincurchin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon generates electricity when it digests food. It uses its five hard teeth to scrape seaweed off surfaces and eat it.

Name: Clefairy
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefable
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Abra
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

Name: Kadabra
Flavor-Text: Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

Name: Alakazam
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

Name: Slowpoke
Flavor-Text: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

Name: Slowbro
Flavor-Text: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.

Name: Gastly
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Haunter
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Gengar
Effects: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

Name: Gengar ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand.

Name: Drowzee
Flavor-Text: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children’s are much tastier.

Name: Hypno
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes -20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

Name: Jynx
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: Discard 2 [P] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It was created by a scientist after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA-engineering experiments.

Name: Mewtwo ex
Effects: Discard 2 [P] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

Name: Kirlia
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Gardevoir
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take 1 [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to the [P] Pokémon in the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

Name: Woobat
Flavor-Text: While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it’s evidence that Woobat live there.

Name: Swoobat
Flavor-Text: Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won’t be able to fly for a little while.

Name: Golett
Flavor-Text: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

Name: Golurk
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

Name: Sandshrew
Flavor-Text: It loves to bathe in the grit of dry, sandy areas. By sand bathing, the Pokémon rids itself of dirt and moisture clinging to its body.

Name: Sandslash
Flavor-Text: The drier the area Sandslash lives in, the harder and smoother the Pokémon’s spikes will feel when touched.

Name: Diglett
Flavor-Text: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

Name: Mankey
Flavor-Text: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

Name: Primeape
Effects: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

Name: Machoke
Flavor-Text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

Name: Machamp
Flavor-Text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

Name: Geodude
Flavor-Text: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

Name: Graveler
Flavor-Text: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

Name: Golem
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

Name: Onix
Flavor-Text: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

Name: Cubone
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.
Flavor-Text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

Name: Marowak
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

Name: Marowak ex
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

Name: Hitmonlee
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.

Name: Hitmonchan
Flavor-Text: Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.

Name: Rhyhorn
Flavor-Text: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

Name: Rhydon
Flavor-Text: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

Name: Kabuto
Flavor-Text: This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.

Name: Kabutops
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.

Name: Mienfoo
Flavor-Text: In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

Name: Mienshao
Flavor-Text: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.

Name: Clobbopus
Flavor-Text: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

Name: Grapploct
Effects: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

Name: Ekans
Flavor-Text: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

Name: Arbok
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.

Name: Nidoran♀
Effects: Put 1 random Nidoran♂ from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Females are more sensitive to smells than males. While foraging, they’ll use their whiskers to check wind direction and stay downwind of predators.

Name: Nidorina
Flavor-Text: The horn on its head has atrophied. It’s thought that this happens so Nidorina’s children won’t get poked while their mother is feeding them.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Nidoking.
Flavor-Text: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.

Name: Nidoran♂
Flavor-Text: The horn on a male Nidoran’s forehead contains a powerful poison. This is a very cautious Pokémon, always straining its large ears.

Name: Nidorino
Flavor-Text: With a horn that’s harder than diamond, this Pokémon goes around shattering boulders as it searches for a moon stone.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.

Name: Zubat
Flavor-Text: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

Name: Golbat
Flavor-Text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

Name: Muk
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

Name: Koffing
Flavor-Text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

Name: Weezing
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

Name: Bisharp
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

Name: Meltan
Effects: Take 1 [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

Name: Melmetal
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes -20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

Name: Dratini
Flavor-Text: It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

Name: Dragonair
Flavor-Text: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

Name: Dragonite
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 4 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.
Flavor-Text: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.

Name: Pidgey
Flavor-Text: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

Name: Pidgeotto
Flavor-Text: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

Name: Pidgeot
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

Name: Rattata
Flavor-Text: Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

Name: Raticate
Flavor-Text: People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.

Name: Spearow
Flavor-Text: Its reckless nature leads it to stand up to others—even large Pokémon—if it has to protect its territory.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

Name: Jigglypuff
Flavor-Text: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

Name: Wigglytuff
Flavor-Text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

Name: Wigglytuff ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Draw 1 card.
Flavor-Text: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

Name: Persian
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

Name: Farfetch'd
Flavor-Text: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

Name: Doduo
Flavor-Text: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a  sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

Name: Dodrio
Flavor-Text: An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads—even for a second—will get pecked severely.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Chansey
Flavor-Text: This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.

Name: Tauros
Flavor-Text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

Name: Ditto
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can’t maintain its disguise.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Porygon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

Name: Aerodactyl
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon back into their deck.
Flavor-Text: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.

Name: Snorlax
Flavor-Text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Name: Minccino
Flavor-Text: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

Name: Wooloo
Flavor-Text: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

Name: Dubwool
Flavor-Text: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You’ll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

Name: Helix Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Dome Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Old Amber
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Erika
Description: Heal 50 damage from 1 of your [G] Pokémon.

Name: Misty
Description: Choose 1 of your [W] Pokémon, and flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.

Name: Blaine
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Ninetales, Rapidash, or Magmar do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Koga
Description: Put your Muk or Weezing in the Active Spot into your hand.

Name: Giovanni
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Brock
Description: Take a [F] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to Golem or Onix.

Name: Sabrina
Description: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

Name: Lt. Surge
Description: Move all [L] Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Raichu, Electrode, or Electabuzz in the Active Spot.

Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

Name: Gloom
Flavor-Text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

Name: Rapidash
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

Name: Squirtle
Flavor-Text: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

Name: Lapras
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

Name: Electrode
Flavor-Text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

Name: Alakazam
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

Name: Slowpoke
Flavor-Text: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

Name: Diglett
Flavor-Text: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

Name: Cubone
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.
Flavor-Text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Nidoking.
Flavor-Text: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.

Name: Golbat
Flavor-Text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

Name: Weezing
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

Name: Venusaur ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

Name: Exeggutor ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

Name: Charizard ex
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Arcanine ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

Name: Moltres ex
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Take an amount of [R] Energy from your Energy Zone equal to the number of heads and attach it to your Benched [R] Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Blastoise ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Articuno ex
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Pikachu ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon.

Name: Zapdos ex
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

Name: Gengar ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand.

Name: Mewtwo ex
Effects: Discard 2 [P] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Marowak ex
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

Effects: Discard 2 [P] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Mew
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
